CAMPAIGN 1
(AD SET TESTING)
Audience Testing Cheatsheet -
Grad Testing Phase 1
GOAL: Find winning audiences
1. Identify your 5W Avatar audiences and angles to
build your ad sets.
2. Budget: At least $20 per ad set
(e.g. 5 ad sets = $100) - if budget limited, then limit the
number of ad sets tested at any given time
3. Set a MINIMUM budget at the ad set level = [total
budget / number of ad sets] / 2
(e.g. If our total CBO campaign budget is $500 for 10 ad
sets, then [500/10]/2 = $25 minimum spend per ad set)
4. Create ads and test 3 at any given time per ad set
unless your ad set budgets are higher than $100 per ad
set per day in which case go up to 5 or 6. If you only
have one ad, duplicate it 3 times into the ad set
5. Share the same 3 ads from above into each ad set
using shared Post IDs (for post types that support this).
6. Test. You should expect results within 48-72 hours
(depending on budget and achieving at least 2.5K
impressions per ad).

Notes:
Once we've found winning ads (and thus winning
audiences) we shift the ad set into Phase 2 because
this phase 1 is all about finding winner audiences.
As a rough guide, for Ecom I use 'Add to Cart' (ATC) or
'Initiate Checkout' (IC) pixel events for testing - a
compromise between quality of the 'event' and cost
(CPM). I am looking for a good cost per ATC or IC
within my 2.5K impressions. If testing for Purchases
then aim for around 5K impressions to build enough
data.
For lead generation I generally use the lead objective
such as Leads or Registrations for testing and aim for
around 1K impressions per ad to look at results.
Winning ad sets shift into new testing CBOs (not the
CBOs tested here) so we can lock those in place and
focus on creative testing.
They also move into our Prospecting campaigns ready
to see if we can build up spend and profitability.
www.getwsodo.com
